Title: Two types of O-methyltransferase are involved in biosynthesis of anticancer methoxylated 4′-deoxyflavones in Scutellaria baicalensis Georgi

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: O-methylation of adjacent hydroxyl groups on flavones
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to one of two adjacent hydroxyl groups on flavones, catalyzing C6 methylation of baicalein to form oroxylin A, C8 methylation of compound 18 to form tenaxin II, and 3′-O-methylation of luteolin to form chrysoeriol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation/formation of mono-methoxylated flavones including oroxylin A, tenaxin II, and chrysoeriol
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA-treated roots profiled, but induction specifically highlighted mainly for SbPFOMT5
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast feeding assays; in vitro enzyme assays with purified protein; LC-MS/HPLC product identification
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering production of methoxylated flavones such as oroxylin A and tenaxin II
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression profiling (FPKM); metabolomics by UPLC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inactive or very weak on chrysin and apigenin; acts on substrates with adjacent hydroxyl groups
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbPFOMT2 methylates baicalein to produce oroxylin A. 2. It methylates compound 18 to produce tenaxin II and luteolin to produce chrysoeriol. 3. It is root-expressed and participates in root methoxylated flavone biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: wogonin; oroxylin A; tenaxin II; chrysoeriol; skullcapflavone I; tenaxin I
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: methoxylated flavones with enhanced anticancer/pro-apoptotic potential
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: wogonin; oroxylin A; tenaxin II; chrysoeriol; 6,8-dimethoxy intermediate; with partner enzymes enables skullcapflavone I and tenaxin I
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers methyl groups to adjacent hydroxyl positions on flavones, including C8 of norwogonin to form wogonin, C6 of baicalein to form oroxylin A, C8 of compound 18 to form tenaxin II, and 3′-O-methylation of luteolin to form chrysoeriol; in combination with Type I OMTs supports multi-methoxylated flavone formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product; forms intermediate for higher methoxylated products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: principal activity driving wogonin synthesis in vivo in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major root PFOMT promoting formation of multiple mono- and polymethoxylated flavones and enabling biosynthesis of skullcapflavone I and tenaxin I in yeast combinations
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in roots; significantly induced by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA induces expression; yeast substrate-feeding conditions used for product formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: previous and current yeast in vivo assays; purified enzyme in vitro assays; co-expression in yeast; LC-MS/HPLC; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for producing anticancer methoxylated flavones in S. baicalensis root cultures or yeast; useful in combinatorial pathway design
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression profiling (FPKM); metabolomics by UPLC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts on substrates with adjacent hydroxyl groups; weak/no activity on chrysin and apigenin; principal in vivo enzyme for wogonin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbPFOMT5 catalyzes methylation of several flavones with adjacent hydroxyl groups to produce wogonin, oroxylin A, tenaxin II and chrysoeriol. 2. It is highly expressed in roots and induced by MeJA, supporting a key role in root methoxylated flavone biosynthesis. 3. Co-expression with SbFOMT6 yields skullcapflavone I and with SbFOMT5 yields tenaxin I in yeast.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Scutellaria baicalensis; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: methoxylated 4′-deoxyflavone intermediate in specialized flavone metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to the C7-hydroxyl group of baicalein
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate related to methoxylated flavone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically methylates baicalein at C7 to generate 7-methoxybaicalein; no detectable activity on norwogonin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highest in stems, followed by roots, leaves and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast feeding assay; in vitro purified enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; LC-MS/HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used for selective C7 methylation of baicalein
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely less relevant than root-preferential OMTs for root metabolite engineering due to stem-biased expression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows highest catalytic efficiency toward baicalein among tested FOMTs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbFOMT3 is a baicalein-specific 7-O-methyltransferase. 2. It produces 7-methoxybaicalein in yeast and in vitro. 3. It has the highest catalytic efficiency toward baicalein but is stem-biased in expression.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Scutellaria baicalensis; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: methoxylated flavones with enhanced bioactivity; contributes to anticancer polymethoxylated flavones
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: baicalein; norwogonin; monohydroxy 4′-deoxyflavones; 5,6,7,8-pentahydroxyflavone (with SbPFOMT5 in pathway design)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 7-methoxybaicalein; oroxylin A; 5-hydroxy-6,7-dimethoxyflavone; 6-hydroxy-5,7-dimethoxyflavone; isowogonin; with SbPFOMT5 produces tenaxin I
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Primarily transfers a methyl group to the C7-hydroxyl of baicalein and norwogonin, but uniquely can also methylate C5 and C6 hydroxyl positions of baicalein and monohydroxy 4′-deoxyflavones
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product; forms intermediate; completes polymethoxylated end product with partner enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A multifunctional Type I OMT that generates several methoxylated baicalein derivatives and, together with SbPFOMT5, enables tenaxin I biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in roots and JA-treated roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: JA-treated roots show high transcript accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast feeding assays; purified enzyme assays; kinetic analysis; substrate range assays; structural modeling and mutagenesis; co-expression in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important engineering enzyme for generating diverse methoxylated 4′-deoxyflavones, especially tenaxin I
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad regioselectivity may generate product mixtures rather than a single compound
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unusual multi-site specificity is supported by structural modeling and mutant analysis of hydrophobic-hole residues
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbFOMT5 mainly catalyzes C7 methylation but can also methylate C5 and C6 positions of baicalein. 2. It converts norwogonin to isowogonin. 3. Together with SbPFOMT5 it produces tenaxin I in yeast and is root/JA enriched.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Scutellaria baicalensis; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 7-methoxybaicalein; isowogonin; with SbPFOMT5 produces skullcapflavone I
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to the C7-hydroxyl group of baicalein and norwogonin; in combination with SbPFOMT5 enables dual methylation of compound 18 to produce skullcapflavone I
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product; completes end product with partner enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes C7 methylation of baicalein and norwogonin and, with SbPFOMT5, enables efficient skullcapflavone I production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in roots and JA-treated roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: JA-treated roots show high transcript accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast feeding assays; purified enzyme assays; kinetic analysis; combinatorial yeast pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for selective C7 methylation and for engineering skullcapflavone I biosynthesis in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: More efficient than SbFOMT5 for norwogonin; structural basis unresolved by current docking model
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbFOMT6 is a 7-O-methyltransferase active on both baicalein and norwogonin. 2. It generates isowogonin from norwogonin. 3. Co-expression with SbPFOMT5 efficiently produces skullcapflavone I in yeast.
